"module 1: define the adjacency matrix and matrix exponential  
the adjacency matrix A represents the graph, with weights as citations. the matrix exponential e^A calculates the expected number of visits to each vertex in a continuous-time random walk. <<the>> <<matrix>> <<exponential>> <<calculates>> <<the>> <<expected>> <<number>> <<of>> <<visits>> <<to>> <<each>> <<vertex>> <<in>> <<a>> <<continuous-time>> <<random>> <<walk>>.  

* * *  

module 2: apply rank-reduction with SVD  
the singular value decomposition of A decomposes it into U, S, V. the low-rank matrix B_k is formed by keeping only the top k singular values. <<the>> <<singular>> <<value>> <<decomposition>> <<of>> <<A>> <<decomposes>> <<it>> <<into>> <<U>> <<,>> <<S>> <<,>> <<V>> <<.>> <<the>> <<low-rank>> <<matrix>> <<B_k>> <<is>> <<formed>> <<by>> <<keeping>> <<only>> <<the>> <<top>> <<k>> <<singular>> <<values>>.  

* * *  

module 3: validate the rank-reduction method  
the Frobenius norm of A - B_k is minimized by retaining the top k singular values. <<the>> <<Frobenius>> <<norm>> <<of>> <<A>> <<->> <<B_k>> <<is>> <<minimized>> <<by>> <<retaining>> <<the>> <<top>> <<k>> <<singular>> <<values>>.>> <<the>> <<method>> <<is>> <<valid>> <<as>> <<it>> <<minimizes>> <<the>> <<Frobenius>> <<norm>> <<of>> <<A>> <<->> <<B_k>> <<.>> <<the>> <<result>> <<is>> <<a>> <<low-rank>> <<approximation>> <<of>> <<A>> <<.>>"
